verdict_reasoning: SRC is a cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically resident in the cytoplasm and associated with inner-leaflet-anchored lipid-modified pools via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. However, SRC traffics to the PM as part of receptor-mediated signaling complexes during growth-factor / cytokine activation and is transiently recruited to activated receptor tyrosine kinases (EGF receptors, integrins, etc.). SRC also relocates to the outer leaflet during cell-death-induced surface exposure (apoptosis, necroptosis) and oncogenic transformation in cancer cells. The baseline state is inner-leaflet anchored, but documented non-baseline surface pools via activation-induced and cancer-state-induced display push this to contextual.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Extent to which SRC PM pool during normal RTK activation reaches the outer leaflet versus remaining predominantly on the inner leaflet during signaling-complex assembly. Also uncertain whether cancer-cell ecto-display of SRC is routine or limited to specific oncogenic contexts.
